headline: Owl direction abandoned. v3 minimalism: low symmetric bathtub, two-tier fillet schedule, no decoration. Cradle volume drops from ~207 cm³ (v2) to ~131 cm³ (v3).

dimensions.notes: Mesh Z-span = 28.001 (-3 feet to +25 wall top). Echoed cradle.z = 28 matches mesh bbox z-span (the report includes the foot extension below the build-plate datum). All four perimeter walls are 25 mm uniform — no tall element exists anywhere.
Tray is unchanged from round 2 (Fix 7 of modeler-notes-v3 — leave alone). Verified clean (no leftover face geometry from round 1).

specDeviations.reason: v3 increases foot_inset from 5 mm to 12 mm to (a) accommodate the foot-to-plate r=1.5 upper blend without overshooting the base plate footprint (with r=10 base-plate corner fillets, the r=1.5 blend cone diameter of 11 mm needs to clear the corner radius), and (b) keep the foot under the base plate body (not crowning the rounded corner). Round 2 had foot_blend_r=1.0 + foot_inset=5, which barely fit; with v3's r=1.5 blend, inset must grow.
Per Fix 5 of modeler-notes-v3: bumped from r=8 to r=10 to consolidate hero radii. r=10 fits within the 11 mm side-step (verified by assert).
Per Fix 6 of modeler-notes-v3: bumped from r=8 to r=10 to consolidate as the hero radius. Applied to all 4 base plate corners, top and bottom of plate.

Per Fix 4 of modeler-notes-v3: cradle exterior vertical corner fillets bump from r=4 to r=10 hero. Applied via wall_footprint() = base_footprint() — same outer offset, so the wall and base share the same exterior outline (continuous solid).

Per Fix 3 of modeler-notes-v3: top-edge fillet bumped from r=1.5 to r=3 utility, applied via the quarter-arc stack in low_wall_block_solid(). The brief's two-tier schedule sets r=3 as the named utility radius.
Per Fix 3 of modeler-notes-v3: foot-to-plate upper blend r=1.5 is the documented function-driven exception (foot bottom stays flat for FDM first-layer adhesion).

fix1_back_panel_collapsed_to_25mm_low_wa.result: The -Y wall is now a 25 mm low wall identical to the +Y, +X, -X walls. All four perimeter walls are 25 mm uniform. The cable notch (z=0..20) leaves a 5 mm bridge above (z=20..25, 25 mm wide, well within FDM bridging tolerance). Wall thickness 3 mm uniform on all 4 perimeter walls.

fix2_all_owl_face_tuft_geometry_deleted.result: Zero face / eye / beak / tuft / disc / panel geometry exists in v3.

utility_r3: Top edges of all 4 perimeter walls (rolled via quarter-arc stack)
All break-edges (any wall→wall and wall→plate transition not in the hero list)
Tray exterior vertical corner edges (already r=3 in tray.scad — unchanged)
hero_r10: Cradle exterior corner vertical edges (4 corners, full wall height) — delivered via offset(r=10) offset(r=-10) on the stepped footprint
Base plate corners (top and bottom, all 4 corners) — same hero offset on the base footprint
Printer-section → shelf concave transition fillet on both ±X sides (bumped 8 → 10)
exception_r1_5: Foot-to-plate upper blend (foot bottom stays flat for FDM first-layer adhesion)
exception_r2: Tray scoop lip leading edge (kept from round 2; documented in spec.json)

fix3_fillet_schedule_consolidated_to_two.no_chamfers: verified — no chamfer_cylinder usage; no 45° bevels; concave fillet at printer→shelf is a circular arc, not a chamfer.
fix3_fillet_schedule_consolidated_to_two.no_mid_tier_radii: verified — only r=3, r=10, r=1.5, r=2 anywhere in the cradle + tray (and r=2 is intentional documented exception).

fix4_cradle_exterior_corner_r10.scad_module: wall_footprint() and base_footprint() share offset(r=10) offset(r=-10) of stepped_footprint_raw()
fix4_cradle_exterior_corner_r10.result: All 4 cradle exterior corners (where ±X walls meet ±Y walls) are r=10 vertical fillets running the full 25 mm wall height. At the top, where the hero r=10 vertical fillet meets the utility r=3 horizontal top fillet, the offset-of-offset stack approximates a sphere-blend triple-corner naturally (no separate triple-corner construction needed).

fix5_printer_to_shelf_concave_fillet_r10.geometry: Concave quarter-arcs on both ±X sides at the printer→shelf step. Tangent points: vertical-tangent on printer side wall at (x_printer_wall, printer_section_d - r); horizontal-tangent on the y=printer_section_d step line at (x_printer_wall + r, printer_section_d). r=10 fits within the 11 mm side_step (verified by assert).
fix5_printer_to_shelf_concave_fillet_r10.fitment_note: side_step (11) - r (10) = 1 mm sliver between arc end and shelf wall — absorbed by the hero corner fillet on the shelf wall.

fix6_base_plate_corners_r10.result: All 4 base plate corners (top and bottom of plate) are r=10 hero. Same hero offset as wall_footprint() — base and wall share the same exterior outline so the cradle reads as a continuous solid.

fix7_tray_unchanged.result: tray.scad untouched in v3. Verified clean — no face/owl geometry. Tray spec params unchanged.

fix8_host_object_proxy_kept.result: Module + render_with_host parameter survive v3 reduction. Used for in-use renders via -D render_with_host=true.

renders_produced: output/cradle-user-front-threequarter.png (bare-part record showing fillet schedule landing)
output/cradle-user-top-threequarter.png (top-down full-perimeter symmetry confirmation — REPLACES round-2 user-left view)
fix9_render_set_updated.render_method: Direct openscad invocation with --viewall --autocenter and explicit 6-tuple cameras. cli-anything-openscad presets remain unusable per round-1 + round-2 lessons.

features.name: base_plate
features.description: Stepped base plate with r=10 hero corner radius (top and bottom of plate, all 4 corners). Footprint follows stepped_footprint_raw — narrow under the printer section (X = 11..97, Y = 0..160), wider under the shelf (X = 0..108, Y = 160..254.9). Concave r=10 quarter-arc on both ±X sides at the printer→shelf transition.

features.name: corner_feet
features.description: Four cylindrical feet (d=8, h=3) inset 12 mm from base plate corners. Foot bottom is flat (FDM first-layer adhesion). Upper r=1.5 conic flare blends foot to plate (function-driven fillet exception).

features.name: low_wall_block
features.description: 25 mm tall stepped perimeter wall block. All 4 perimeter walls uniform 25 mm — no tall element anywhere. Wall thickness 3 mm uniform. Top edge r=3 utility fillet rolled via quarter-arc stack. Exterior vertical corner edges r=10 hero (delivered via the wall_footprint offset round-trip). Carved interior provides the printer pocket (80 × 154 × 25 mm visible) and the tray slot opening (open-top + open-front).

features.name: printer_shelf_concave_fillet
features.description: r=10 concave quarter-arc on both ±X sides where the 86 mm printer section transitions to the 108 mm shelf section. The one sculptural move on the cradle proper. Self-supporting (concave with material on the inside of the curve, no overhang).

features.name: cable_slot_cutter
features.description: U-notch cut in the -Y back wall (against-wall face). 25 mm wide × 20 mm tall, centered at x = 54 (cradle center). Z range 0..20. Wall above the notch (z = 20..25, 5 mm tall × 25 mm wide) prints as a short bridge between the side walls of the notch — within FDM bridging tolerance.

features.name: host_object_proxy
features.description: RENDER-ONLY printer proxy box (78 × 152 × 143 mm) at the installed position inside the printer pocket (X=15..93, Y=4..156, Z=4..147). Gated by render_with_host parameter — default false (excluded from STL). PNG renders use -D render_with_host=true for the use-state hero shots.

featuresTray.name: tray_shell
featuresTray.description: Rectangular bin, 103.2 × 94.2 × 21.6 mm exterior, 100 × 91 × 20 mm interior. Walls 1.6 mm, floor 1.6 mm. Open top. Four vertical exterior corners filleted r=3.

featuresTray.name: scoop_lip
featuresTray.description: Upper 14 mm of the tray's +Y (user-front) wall tilts back at 45°. Lower 7 mm stays vertical as structural base. Leading edge r=2 (intentional sharp-ish-but-not-sharp documented exception).

featuresTray.name: integrated_finger_grip
featuresTray.description: Concave cylindrical dip (R≈46 mm) in the central 30 mm of the scoop face. 2.5 mm depth perpendicular to the scoop face. Serves as a finger-pull for tray removal — merged into the scoop, not a separate feature.

cradle.bed_face: base plate bottom (z = -3 flat feet contact bed)
cradle.notes: v3 has zero supports-permitted features. The 5 mm bridge above the cable notch is the only feature requiring printability attention; 25 mm bridge span is within FDM bridging tolerance for PLA. No overhang exceeds 45° at any visible feature.

supports_permitted_but_avoidable: scoop_lip 45° face (printable as overhang per round-1 verification)

userOrientationConfirmed.user_front: +Y face (tray slides in/out here; printer faces user)

quietFrameVerdict.details: In cradle-user-front-in-use.png the printer proxy (143 mm tall) dominates the silhouette as intended; the cradle (28 mm including feet) is a small low frame around the printer base. The cradle does not compete with the printer — it reads as 'printer perched in a quiet rectangular tray' per the brief's composition_metaphor. No face, no decoration, no tall back panel competes for attention. The threequarter in-use render confirms the same composition from a 3/4 angle: printer towering, cradle a small frame at the base, tray slot in front.

filletConsistencyVerdict.details: Every visible exterior edge of the cradle is r=3 (utility, top edges of perimeter walls) or r=10 (hero, exterior corners + base plate corners + printer→shelf concave). The two function-driven exceptions are documented in spec: r=1.5 foot-to-plate upper blend (flat foot bottom for adhesion), r=2 tray scoop leading edge (intentional sharp-ish-but-not-sharp). No chamfered edges anywhere. No mid-tier radii (4, 5, 6, 8) anywhere.

uncertainItemNotes.r10_corner_read: After rendering, the r=10 cradle exterior corners read clean — not bulbous, not cartoony. The 25 mm wall height is roughly 2.5x the corner radius, so the corners blend continuously into the walls without the corner radius dominating. Recommend keeping at r=10. If the print-reviewer disagrees, a drop to r=6 would be straightforward (single param change).
uncertainItemNotes.r10_uniform_hero_radius: The same r=10 applied to corners + base plate + printer→shelf concave reads as a coherent 'soft footprint' language rather than feeling repetitive. The three contexts are visually distinct (vertical corners vs. plate corners vs. concave sweep), so the shared radius reads as 'consistent design language' rather than 'three identical features'.
uncertainItemNotes.bridge_above_cable_notch: 5 mm wall × 25 mm bridge span. The bridge spans between the two side walls of the cable notch (each 41.5 mm of solid wall material on either side of the 25 mm notch — strong anchors). PLA bridges 25 mm reliably without supports. No print-reviewer flag necessary, but listed in testPrintCandidates for verification.
uncertainItemNotes.top_edge_fillet_r3_legibility: r=3 top edge fillet is 12% of the 25 mm wall height. In the renders the fillet is visible but subtle — it does not 'roll' the wall. Reads as a deliberate softening, not as a manufacturing artifact. Keep at r=3.
